experiences.intentions: The board is empty except for the active Z near the top in its spawn orientation, so there are no immediate threats and lots of freedom. The goal now is to set up a flat, clean stack for upcoming I,O,T,J pieces. On an empty board, moving the Z toward a side before dropping is best; placing it on the left keeps the center open and avoids awkward central bumps. Candidate actions: hard_drop now would lock it near the center unnecessarily; soft_drop advances downward but commits without improving placement; rotations are not needed on an empty field; right would mirror the side-placement plan, but left is a solid first step toward edge stacking.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Current piece is a Z near the top center in its horizontal orientation. The board is otherwise empty, so there are no immediate threats; the opportunity is to place pieces cleanly to keep the stack flat and preserve space for the upcoming I, O, T, J pieces. With an empty board, the best sub-goal is to get the Z to the floor quickly rather than spend turns shifting unnecessarily. Among candidates, left/right only reposition without urgency, rotation is not needed on an empty board, soft_drop advances only one row, while hard_drop safely places the piece at the bottom immediately and minimizes wasted turns.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active I piece is horizontal near the top, while the stack at the bottom is just a small Z/S bump with plenty of open space. Goal is to place the I efficiently for future line clears and avoid leaving it stranded horizontally high up. Rotating vertical now creates a flexible central well placement and is safe because there are no nearby collisions. Compared with moving or dropping immediately, rotate_right improves the piece’s shape first and preserves options on subsequent turns.

experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: The active J piece is high near the spawn area with a mostly flat stack below except for a shallow central structure: Z at the bottom, I across above, O above that, and T above, leaving broad open space on both sides and no immediate danger. experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active L is high near columns 5-7 with a mostly empty field above a developed stack on the lower left/center. Immediate threat is low because the piece has plenty of space. Goal is to place the L to support a flatter surface and avoid creating overhangs over the central stack. Moving left starts bringing the piece toward the uneven left-center structure where an L can likely tuck in better on future moves; rotating or dropping now is premature since there is lots of vertical space and no urgent lock-in. Among candidates, left improves alignment potential most safely this turn.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active L is high and centered-right with a relatively flat stack top around columns 2-7 but a left-side well structure from the J. Immediate threat is low because the board is far from the top. Goal is to place the L efficiently without creating holes; the cleanest near-term sub-goal is to move it toward the left/middle where it can later complement the uneven surface, rather than hard-dropping early onto the right and building height over the O/T area. Among actions, left improves future placement flexibility, right shifts toward a less useful high area, rotations are not yet necessary this high up, and drops commit too early. So move left now.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is an L near the top with a mostly open field above a jagged stack. The main opportunity is to guide it toward the left-side cavity/step so later L,J,Z,O sequence can stack cleanly without creating holes. Immediate hard drop is premature from spawn because horizontal L would land high on the central-right surface; moving left now preserves flexibility and aims for the open left channel. experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active L is high near center-left. Surface below has a left well around columns 0-2 and a flat-ish shelf on row 17 from J and I, with clutter around cols 2-5. experiences.intentions: Active L is high on an otherwise open upper field. Surface below has a low left structure and a flat-ish right area, with no immediate threat of topping out. Goal is to place the L efficiently while preserving a clean stack and avoiding awkward overhangs. Candidate moves: hard_drop now would place the current horizontal L around columns 1-3 on top of the left stack, which is reasonable but may be improved by shifting. Rotating now could create a vertical L for tucking, but there is ample time and no urgency. Best immediate sub-goal is to position toward the left before committing.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is an L near the upper-left stack. Current orientation already fits; rotate_right would also fit but doesn't improve immediate placement. The surface below has support under the current columns, and moving left is likely blocked by wall/stack while moving right risks creating overhangs above the S/T area. Goal is to place the L cleanly without opening holes; dropping now locks it into a compact left-side contour and preserves the center for upcoming I/J/Z/O. soft_drop advances safely, but hard_drop secures the good placement immediately.

experiences.intentions: Active L is high near center-left in horizontal orientation. Surface below has a central T bump around columns 3-5 and lower stacks on the left; dropping now would be premature from this height, so we should reposition first. Goal is to place L to smooth the left/center surface and avoid creating holes. Moving left sets up the L to approach the left side where it can later rotate/drop into a more useful notch; right heads toward the O/I stack and seems less compatible. Rotation now is possible but less urgent than horizontal alignment. Best immediate action: shift left.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is a T at rows 13-14 centered around columns 3-5, resting above the OO block and near a left-side cavity. Current stack is low with no immediate danger, so the goal is to place the T to improve surface and potentially set up future clears with upcoming J,Z,O,S. Moving left would shift the T toward the uneven left structure and can help fill the notch above the left stack; moving right drifts toward the flatter I/OO area but is less useful and may create overhangs. experiences.intentions: Board has a relatively flat center with a left-side stack and an active T already in a useful horizontal orientation resting above mid-left. Rotations other than current are invalid here, so main options are move, drop, or keep. Goal is to place the T to reduce surface awkwardness and avoid creating holes; shifting right can set up a cleaner tuck over the center-left/center area before dropping on a later turn, while dropping immediately would likely lock in a less flexible spot. Left is constrained by the existing stack, rotation is unavailable, and no_op wastes tempo. Best sub-goal now is improve horizontal alignment before committing. Therefore move right.

experiences.intentions: The active J piece is high near the center-top with plenty of time before lock. The stack below is uneven but not dangerously high; the main opportunity is to place J efficiently without creating holes while setting up for upcoming Z,O,S,I. Current J orientation is flat-top (rotation 0). A good immediate sub-goal is to move this J toward the left side where there is a notch above the L/S structure, and starting lateral movement now is better than dropping early from spawn height. Candidate actions: hard_drop/soft_drop commit too early before alignment; rotations are not yet necessary because the current orientation can still be repositioned later; right moves away from the useful left-side placements. Therefore the best first action is to move left.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is a Z near the top, currently horizontal around columns 4-7. The stack below is low enough that there is time to position it. Surface has a left-center notch around rows 18-19 at columns 3-4, where a horizontal Z could fit well if moved left. Immediate goal is to steer the piece toward a clean landing that reduces holes and avoids creating awkward overhangs. Candidate actions: hard_drop now would place it badly on the far right/top; soft_drop commits too early before alignment; rotate_right makes it vertical but the current board seems to favor keeping it horizontal for the left-center slot; right moves away from the target. Moving left starts guiding the Z toward that notch while preserving flexibility for later drop/rotation.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is a Z near the top, currently horizontal at columns 3-5. The stack below is low-to-mid with a noticeable vertical trench around column 3 and relatively flat open air above, so there is no immediate threat. Goal is to place the Z efficiently without creating unnecessary overhangs, ideally moving toward the left-side contour where a Z can fit later; but from the current height, the first priority is simply to advance the piece downward while keeping options open. Rotating now is unnecessary, and lateral movement can be delayed because there is ample space. Soft dropping improves progress while preserving future placement flexibility better than hard dropping.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is Z near the top with plenty of space. Surface below has a noticeable cavity around the center-left, and immediate threats are low because stack height is moderate. Goal is to place the Z efficiently to reduce future holes while setting up for upcoming O,S,I,T pieces. Candidate actions: moving left would align the Z over the left-side notch structure better than current spawn; moving right drifts toward flatter but less useful space; rotating vertical this high is possible but not yet necessary; dropping now would commit to a mediocre placement. Best sub-goal is to start shifting the Z left toward the center-left gap.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is S at the very top, currently horizontal with open space below. Surface has a left-center notch around rows 11-13 where an S can fit well; immediate threats are low since stack is far from top. Goal is to place the S efficiently to reduce holes and keep the center manageable. Candidate actions: hard_drop would commit immediately near the spawn column, likely not ideal without horizontal alignment; soft_drop just lowers without improving alignment; rotations are unnecessary since current horizontal S is already useful; right moves away from the existing notch. Moving left starts steering the piece toward the left-center cavity for a cleaner placement.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is an S near the top with ample horizontal space. The stack below is uneven, with a left-center notch around rows 11-14 and relatively open top. Goal is to place the S to improve surface smoothness and avoid creating overhangs while setting up upcoming I/T/J. Candidate actions: soft_drop/hard_drop would commit immediately in the current column, likely too early without alignment; rotations are possible but not yet necessary because the spawn horizontal S can slide into gaps. Moving left targets the left-center depression above the L/S/T area and is safer than right, which heads toward flatter but less useful space. So the best immediate sub-goal is to shift left for better placement options.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is S near the upper-left/center, currently horizontal at rows 4-5. The stack below has a pronounced left-side cavity and uneven surface around columns 1-4, while the right side is lower but not immediately interactable from current spawn without more setup. Goal is to place the S efficiently without creating overhangs, ideally tucking it toward the left depression. Moving left from the current position shifts the S over columns 2-4, which lines it up better with the existing left-side contour and preserves future flexibility. Rotating now makes it narrower but less immediately useful than first shifting into alignment; dropping now would freeze it on a relatively flat high spot and waste placement potential. So the best immediate action is left.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is an I vertical at column 5, spanning rows 3-6. Surface below has moderate bumps, with a deep clean vertical well already around column 1 on the far left and relatively open space near columns 4-6 above the center. Immediate threats are low since stack height is mid-board. Goal is to place the I efficiently without creating holes; best sub-goal is to move it toward the left-side well/flat area rather than hard-dropping into the current central position, which would likely stand high and block future placements. Candidate actions: hard_drop is premature and likely poor centrally; soft_drop commits downward before aligning; rotations only switch between vertical/horizontal, but horizontal at current x would sit high and not yet exploit terrain. Moving left starts repositioning toward the cleaner lane and preserves options for later rotate/drop. So the best current action is left.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is a vertical I at column 4 with open space directly beneath limited by stacked blocks around row 9+. The board has uneven surface with a notable left-side trench, but from current position an immediate hard drop in vertical orientation places the I into the central gap cleanly without risky lateral movement. Goal is to place safely and preserve future flexibility; rotating horizontal here would require moving into a different lane first and is not immediately better. Candidate actions: left/right reposition but delay; rotate changes to horizontal but current columns seem constrained; soft_drop is slower than needed. Best is to lock the I vertically now.

experiences.intentions: Active T is near the top centered around columns 4-6 with a mostly open field above a jagged stack. There is no immediate danger at spawn height, so the goal is to place the T efficiently to preserve flexibility and potentially set up future line clears with upcoming J,Z,I,T. A hard drop now would commit from a weak orientation/column without alignment; lateral movement can wait, but first rotating to the right into the vertical T orientation increases placement options for fitting into notches in the uneven center-left/center stack. Compared with left/right/soft_drop, rotate_right improves future mobility before descent and is low risk.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active T is high near the center-right in rotation 1. Surface below has a vertical well at column 4 and mixed stack; immediate threats are low since piece is near the top with lots of space. Goal is to place T to improve the uneven center without creating overhangs; first sub-goal is align it with the central channel. Moving left shifts the T toward the open center column structure and preserves future rotation/drop options better than dropping now or moving right into less useful space.

experiences.intentions: Active T is near the top in vertical orientation at column 4, with open space directly below only until it reaches the stack around row 11. Immediate goal is to place it efficiently without wasting time; lateral movement could aim for future fit, but from the current high position the safest progress is to advance downward and preserve options for later moves/rotations. hard_drop would commit without full alignment confidence, while soft_drop improves position toward landing and keeps flexibility next turn.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.
